suggestions.title: Finish the dev-public exec terminal isolation fix you already started
suggestions.description: Today’s shell history shows `qzcli exec dev-public nvidia-smi`, and the only live edits in `qzcli_tool` are a fresh-terminal patch plus regression test. Codex can finish and validate that change so `exec` stops hijacking an existing Jupyter terminal.
suggestions.prompt: Finish the `qzcli exec` terminal-isolation patch in the nearby `qzcli_tool` repo: keep the new-terminal behavior in `_exec_launch`, harden it if needed, run the relevant exec tests, and update any docs or changelog notes if the user-facing behavior changed.

suggestions.title: Add a rerun shortcut for today’s stopped H100 smoke job
suggestions.description: Your local `~/.qzcli/jobs.json` shows `qz-smoke-dream342m-bpb-cache-06220644` stopped at 2026-06-22 14:47, followed minutes later by a similar 1xH100 rerun with a hand-edited command. A `create --from-job` or `rerun` flow would remove that copy-paste loop for the next experiment.
suggestions.prompt: In the nearby `qzcli_tool` repo, add a job-cloning workflow for fast experiment reruns: let me start from a tracked job or job ID, override fields like name/command/spec/instances, and submit a new job without manually rebuilding the whole payload. Include tests around loading a prior tracked job and applying overrides.

suggestions.title: Close the last invalid_grant path before more MCP automation hits it
suggestions.description: The repo still has open GitHub issue `#14`, and today’s work is clearly shifting toward Codex/MCP-driven `qzcli` usage. This is worth fixing now because any remaining token fallback will surface as a brittle failure exactly in the automation flow you’re setting up.
suggestions.prompt: Audit the nearby `qzcli_tool` repo for every remaining path that can still hit `/auth/token` for CAS users, reproduce the `invalid_grant` failure from issue #14 where possible, and implement the smallest fix with regression coverage so MCP and CLI flows stay on the cookie/CAS path by default.

suggestions.title: Finish the H200 create defaults patch across CLI and MCP
suggestions.description: On June 22 you changed `cli.py`, `mcp_server.py`, `README.md`, and added `tests/test_create_defaults.py` to make create/job submission default to your H200 profile, but it is still only a local diff. Codex can finish the sweep, remove any leftover old literals, and hand you a commit-ready patch.
suggestions.prompt: Please finish my June 22 create-defaults work in `/Users/wjx/code/qzcli_tool`: verify `qzcli create`, `cmd_batch`, and `qz_create_job` all use the shared H200 defaults consistently, find any remaining old default literals, update any missed docs/tests, and leave me with a clean commit-ready diff.

suggestions.title: Make H200 defaults configurable so H100 submission still stays easy
suggestions.description: The same day you asked Codex about single-card H100 submission, you also hardcoded H200 defaults into the create path. Codex can turn that into a profile or config override so your current H200 workflow stays fast without breaking the H100 path you were checking.
suggestions.prompt: In `/Users/wjx/code/qzcli_tool`, replace the new hardcoded H200 create defaults with a configurable preset mechanism that still defaults to my H200 profile but lets me override to H100 cleanly from config or flags. Update the CLI/MCP paths together, add focused tests, and keep the UX simple for both presets.

suggestions.title: Finish the H200 default-profile change across qzcli create and MCP
suggestions.description: You asked about the qz-cli MCP submit defaults on June 22, and the nearby active repo has uncommitted edits plus a new test around exactly that. Codex can close this loop now by verifying every create path, fixing any remaining mismatches, and leaving you with one authoritative default profile.
suggestions.prompt: In the nearby qzcli_tool repo, finish the in-progress default-profile update for job creation. Verify that `qzcli create`, batch submission defaults, and `qz_create_job` in the MCP server all consistently use the same H200 profile (`H200-3号机房`, the single-card H200 spec, `dev-wjx:gpu1.1`, priority 6), patch any remaining inconsistencies, run the relevant tests, and summarize the final defaults and any behavior changes.
